what: Layout algorithms based on bi- or other partitions — layout_partition
title: Layout algorithms based on bi- or other partitions — layout_partition
text: algorithms layout networks based two partitions, recommended use graphr() {ggraph}. "hierarchy" layout layers first node set along bottom, second node set along top, sequenced spaced necessary minimise edge overlap. "alluvial" layout similar "hierarchy", places successive layers horizontally rather vertically. "railway" layout similar "hierarchy", nodes aligned across layers. "ladder" layout similar "railway", places successive layers horizontally rather vertically. "concentric" layout places "hierarchy" layout around circle, successive layers appearing concentric circles. "multilevel" layout places successive layers multiple levels. "lineage" layout ranks nodes Y axis according values.

title: autograph 1.1.0
text: Fixed labels overlapping nodes (closes #13): labels now keep clear node borders automatically giving ggrepel node’s true rendered size, label_dist adding points-based gap (mirroring igraph’s vertex.label.dist) label_repel = FALSE selecting fixed offset instead repulsion. also fixed pre-existing bug non-repelled labels rendered fully transparent fill package’s theme, making invisible nodes. Node positions now transition seamlessly waves using dynamic stress layout graphlayouts (layout_as_dynamic()), new alpha argument controlling layout stability; layouts computed aggregate network held fixed Changing node composition now handled properly: every node ever appears gets stable position fades place enters exits network New isolates argument ("keep" "fade") controls whether temporarily isolated nodes stay visible fade ; keep_isolates deprecated Dynamic (time-stamped, event-based) networks irps_nuclear now split automatically cumulative time slices via manynet::to_slices(), single dynamic network object passed grapht() works without manual conversion Interval (spell) networks record tie begin/end lifespans, irps_wwi, now split automatically one snapshot per change point showing ties active spell, grapht(irps_wwi) works directly (previously errored networks dynamic carry time attribute to_slices()); irps_wwi now runnable example documentation grapht() now uses dynamic stress layout default even two-mode networks (rather hierarchy layout, collapsed many nodes onto line), suppresses node labels default networks 30 nodes keep frames legible, fades densely overlapping ties read density gradient rather solid mass Fixed error animating networks whose node names contain non-ASCII characters Waves without ties longer silently dropped Closer visual parity graphr(): directed networks get arrowheads segments trimmed target node, signed networks distinguish positive/negative ties linetype colour, mapped aesthetics use palettes factor levels consistent across frames, legends transition along animation Aesthetic-resolution helpers now shared graphr() grapht() (new R/graph_aes.R), styling drift static animated plots Added test suite grapht() (gif rendering required) Now aborts clear message input split waves slices, instead failing much later cryptic igraph error (closes #40); underlying cause — to_waves() silently ignoring time attribute named “wave” — fixed manynet 2.2.2, tutorial example now uses wave attribute, splits correctly manynet 2.2.1 TRUE/"force" uses force-directed bundling, "path" "minimal" selecting non-hierarchical algorithms colour/width/linetype mappings preserved directed networks keep arrowheads. wires ggraph’s non-hierarchical bundling geoms (added ggraph 2.2.0), previously imported never called, ggraph dependency now (>= 2.2.0) Fixed edge_size = 0 fully suppressing edges directed networks (closes #50): arrowhead length hard-coded regardless edge_size, leaving visible arrowhead line hidden. Arrow length now scales resolved edge width (capped heavily-weighted edges don’t get oversized heads) omitted entirely width 0 Fixed two-mode auto-shapes assigning circles second mode: first mode now takes circles second squares, intended Fixed graphr() returning empty plot networks consisting isolates (e.g. empty dyad/triad motifs): isolates now kept whenever removing empty graph Fixed graphs() erroring lists containing tie-less networks (e.g. plot() motif censuses): panels sharing layout now keep isolates every node coordinate every wave Fixed graphr() erroring weight size attributes carrying measure classes (e.g. tie_measure results netrics stored attributes) Fixed vector-recycling warning graphs()’ ego-network detection
